_comment: Connector registry for /mkt-connect. Read-first by design. The flow must honor maxPermission, the secrets rule, and the promotionPath; it must never exceed a connector's cap without completing the promotion gate (human approval + audit).

permissionRules.read: analytics / insights only — safe to auto-enable after a read-only verify call
permissionRules.draft: prepare content or messages but DO NOT send — requires human review of each draft
permissionRules.execute: publish / send / spend — requires explicit human approval + an audit entry PER ACTION

promotionPath._comment: How a connector's permission is raised. A connector never starts above 'read'. Each step is gated; nothing is automatic.
steps: 1. Validate the provider's live API + exact scopes for the intended action (read-only first).
2. Connect at 'read'; confirm with the connector's verify call; record the connection in reports/.
3. To reach 'draft': a human approves; the kit prepares but never sends; each draft is reviewed.
4. To reach 'execute': a human approves PER ACTION; log tool, action, approver, timestamp in reports/.
5. Never exceed maxPermission; raising maxPermission itself is a deliberate, reviewed change to this file.
promotionPath.runtimeNote: Write/execute connectors are 'planned' — live send/spend is NOT wired yet. Until a connector's status is 'available' AND a human has approved execute, treat all write actions as dry-run.

secrets: Secret values live in .env (git-ignored). In .mcp.json, reference them as ${ENV_VAR} only. Never store raw keys in chat, memory, or committed files.

auditSchema._comment: Structured shape for every connector audit entry. Append via the single writer integrations/scripts/audit-append.mjs (one JSONL line per action). An 'execute' action with no approver is REFUSED, not logged. Secrets never appear — log payloadHash, never the payload.
rules: Record a 'draft' or 'execute' action BEFORE performing it; 'execute' requires a non-empty approver.
Never write a secret — record a sha256 payloadHash, not the payload.
audit-verify + a firewall approval-token ship WITH the first promoted execute connector (deferred — see promotionPath.runtimeNote).

connectionState: status:'available' means the connector is SUPPORTED, not that it is wired. Only sage-memory is wired by default (.mcp.json). Before any READ, verify the connector is configured in .mcp.json + .env. If a connector is not wired, every metric it would supply is a DATA GAP — never estimate or fabricate a number.

connectors.name: Google Analytics 4
connectors.verify: fetch one report row for the last 7 days (read-only)

connectors.name: Meta (FB/IG) Ads
connectors.verify: read ad-account name + insights for last 7 days (read-only)

generationProviders._comment: Asset/media generation providers for /mkt-generate-asset + marketing-media-designer (engine: skills/domain/marketing-asset-generation/scripts/generate_asset.py). Distinct from connectors: no read/draft/execute — these are billed content-creation APIs. 'status:available' means the engine SUPPORTS the provider, not that a key is wired; add the key to .env (git-ignored) before use. All hosted image/video generation requires billing (no free tier). Model IDs are env-overridable so preview-model drift is a one-line config change.

providers.name: Google Gemini — Nano Banana + Imagen 4
providers.notes: Nano Banana (fast, editing); set IMAGE_GEN_MODEL=gemini-3-pro-image-preview for high-fidelity in-image text; Imagen 4 for production photographic stills.

providers.name: OpenAI / Codex gen_image (gpt-image)
providers.notes: Same Images API the Codex CLI image tool wraps; called directly for headless use. gpt-image-1 deprecates 2026-10-23. Uses size (mapped from aspect ratio) + quality.

providers.name: Google Veo (video)
providers.notes: 8s clips with native audio; image-to-video via start/end reference frames; async (submit + poll).

providers.name: higgsfield.ai (image + video)
providers.notes: REST submit POST /generations -> poll GET /generations/{id} -> output URL. text/image-to-video, 'Soul'; 5/10s, 480p/720p/1080p. Set HIGGSFIELD_MODEL from the cloud.higgsfield.ai dashboard.

providers.name: MiniMax / Hailuo (optional)
providers.notes: Documented but NOT wired into the engine. Add a provider branch to generate_asset.py to enable image/Hailuo-video/TTS/music.
